# AI Usage Monitor v2.0.1

This maintenance release focuses on making self-updates safer and more
predictable while retaining download-integrity checks.

## Changes

- Complete an intentional shutdown before the update helper replaces and
  relaunches the application.
- Stage and verify the new executable beside the installed copy, replace it
  atomically, and preserve a verified recovery copy until the updated
  application confirms a healthy startup.
- Stop the update if the existing process does not exit within the timeout.
- Roll back and restart the previous version if replacement or the new startup
  fails; a rollback failure never deletes the only known-good backup.
- Avoid holding the shared UI state lock while displaying modal update
  messages.
- Add a Windows updater-helper end-to-end test covering process exit, replacement,
  ready hand-off, relaunch, and transaction cleanup.

## Updating from v2.0.0

Version 2.0.0 can replace the executable on disk but fail to hand over the
running process: the old v2.0.0 process may remain alive and prevent the
restarted v2.0.1 process from taking the single-instance mutex. If the app
does not visibly restart after accepting the in-app update:

1. Choose **Exit** from the AI Usage Monitor tray menu. If the update helper is
   still waiting, it should then complete the replacement and restart the app.
2. If the app does not restart within a few seconds, launch the same
   `ai-usage-monitor.exe` again.
3. Open the tray menu and verify that it reports v2.0.1.
4. If it still reports v2.0.0 or cannot start, close it and install the
   portable ZIP (recommended) or standalone EXE from this release manually.

## Downloads

The initial WinGet package is tracked in
[microsoft/winget-pkgs#400395](https://github.com/microsoft/winget-pkgs/pull/400395).
If WinGet does not find `yinjianxxx.AIUsageMonitor` yet, use the portable ZIP;
it is the recommended manual download because it includes the bilingual
READMEs and retained license and attribution notices. The standalone EXE
remains available for users who prefer a single file. Verify downloads with
the accompanying `SHA256SUMS` file.
